dtcs.description: Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P0ACC (53): Battery Module Temperature Sensor 3 Circuit Low Voltage Three battery module temperature sensors are attached to the battery module, and the motor control module (MCM) continuously monitors the battery module temperature. If the motor control module (MCM) input voltage from battery module temperature sensor 3 is a specified voltage for a set time, a malfunction is detected and a DTC is stored. Three battery module temperature sensors are attached to the battery module, and the motor control module (MCM) continuously monitors the battery module temperature. If the motor control module (MCM) input voltage from battery module temperature sensor 3 is a specified voltage for a set time, a malfunction is detected and a DTC is stored. P0ACD (54) DTC P0ACD: Battery Module Temperature Sensor 3 Circuit High Voltage The motor control module (MCM) calculates the offset value of the battery current sensor during a no voltage condition of the battery module immediately after the ignition switch is turned to ON (II). The MCM converts (analog/digital conversion) the ISOC signal input voltage and when the difference between the maximum value and the minimum value of eight continuous inputs are within 0.015 V, the average value of eight battery current sensor inputs are calculated. When the battery current sensor average value is out of a specified range, a malfunction is detected and a DTC is stored. The motor control module (MCM) controls charge/discharge of the battery module based on the battery current sensor signal. When the battery current sensor ISOC signal voltage is a specified voltage for a set time, the MCM detects a malfunction and stores a DTC. The motor control module (MCM) controls charge/discharge of the battery module based on the battery current sensor signal. When the battery current sensor ISOC signal voltage is a specified voltage for a set time, the MCM detects a malfunction and stores a DTC. Three battery module temperature sensors are attached to the battery module, and the motor control module (MCM) continuously monitors the battery module temperature. If the motor control module (MCM) input voltage from battery module temperature sensor 2 is a specified voltage for a set time, a malfunction is detected and a DTC is stored. Three battery module temperature sensors are attached to the battery module, and the motor control module (MCM) continuously monitors the battery module temperature. If the motor control module (MCM) input voltage from battery module temperature sensor 2 is a specified voltage for a set time, a malfunction is detected and a DTC is stored. When the motor power inverter (MPI) module temperature is a specified value or more in the power save mode, the MPI module temperature decreases if the MPI module cooling system is normal. The MPI module temperature sensor stuck failure is detected by comparing the MPI module temperature decrease over a specified time to a specified value. If the change in MPI module temperature is a specified value within a set time, a malfunction is detected and a DTC is stored. The high voltage contactor between the battery module and the motor power inverter (MPI) module is turned on to activate the integrated motor assist (IMA) system after the ignition switch is turned to ON (II). Before turning the high voltage contactor on, the bypass contactor is turned on, and the PDU capacitor is charged gradually by current limited by the bypass resistor. When the difference between the voltage on the MPI module side and the battery module side is within a set range, the high voltage contactor is turned on, then the bypass contactor is turned off. If the difference between the voltage on the MPI module side and the battery module side is out of a set range for a specified time after the bypass contactor is turned on, a malfunction is detected and a DTC is stored. The motor control module internal communication is done by between the motor control processor and the battery control processor, and between the battery control processor and the high voltage control processor. When each control processor cannot receive the communication data for a specified duration, or received data is abnormal, a malfunction is detected and a DTC is stored. When the high voltage battery detection circuit is a specified voltage for a set time, a malfunction is detected and a DTC is stored. The internal temperature sensor in the motor power inverter (MPI) module detects an overheat and transmits overheating temperature information to the motor control module (MCM) via serial communication. When the MCM receives abnormally high temperature information for a specified duration, a malfunction is detected and a DTC is stored. The motor control module (MCM) obtains the magnetic pole position signal of the motor rotor using the motor rotor position sensor to control motor torque. When an abnormal signal is output from the R/D converter for a set time during each monitoring period, which is repeated, or when the input/output voltage of the motor rotor position sensor is abnormal for a set time, or when the IMA motor speed calculated from the motor rotor position sensor and the engine speed is compared and found to deviate a set amount for a set time, a malfunction is detected and a DTC is stored. The motor control module (MCM) controls the IMA motor output based on the phase motor current sensor signals. When the U phase motor current sensor output voltage is a specified voltage for a set time, a malfunction is detected and a DTC is stored. The motor control module (MCM) controls the IMA motor output based on the phase motor current sensor signals. When the U phase motor current sensor output voltage is a specified voltage for a set time, a malfunction is detected and a DTC is stored. Temperature sensors are built into the battery module to detect a battery module temperature. If the battery module temperature is a specified temperature for a set time, the motor control module (MCM) detects a malfunction in the battery module and stores a DTC. The capacity of the battery module is defined by upper and lower limits of the battery module voltage. When the battery deteriorates, the limits of the battery capacity are narrowed. Under specific driving conditions, the motor control module (MCM) monitors the maximum and minimum voltage of the battery module. The MCM computes the battery module electrical current value during operation to calculate the battery module capacity. If the calculated value is under the specification, a malfunction is detected and a DTC is stored. Three battery module temperature sensors are attached to the battery module, and the motor control module (MCM) continuously monitors the battery module temperature. If the motor control module (MCM) input voltage from battery module temperature sensor 1 is a specified voltage for a set time, a malfunction is detected and a DTC is stored. Three battery module temperature sensors are attached to the battery module, and the motor control module (MCM) continuously monitors the battery module temperature. If the motor control module (MCM) input voltage from battery module temperature sensor 1 is a specified voltage for a set time, a malfunction is detected and a DTC is stored. When the ignition switch is turned to LOCK (0) while the integrated motor assist (IMA) system is in an idle stop condition, the high voltage contactor opens and the stored charge in the power drive unit (PDU) capacitor which is built into the motor power inverter (MPI) module, is consumed by the DC-DC converter and lowers the MPI module voltage. But if there is a high voltage contactor turn-on failure, the discharge mentioned above is impossible. Therefore, when the difference between the MPI module voltage value before the high voltage contactor opening process and the MPI module voltage value after the high voltage contactor opening process does not change for a specified duration, a high voltage contactor turn-on failure is detected and a DTC is stored. The motor control module (MCM) controls the IMA motor output based on the phase motor current sensor signals. When the V phase motor current sensor output voltage is a specified voltage for a set time, a malfunction is detected and a DTC is stored. The motor control module (MCM) controls the IMA motor output based on the phase motor current sensor signals. When the V phase motor current sensor output voltage is a specified voltage for a set time, a malfunction is detected and a DTC is stored. The motor control module (MCM) controls the IMA motor output based on the phase motor current sensor signals. When the W phase motor current sensor output voltage is a specified voltage for a set time, a malfunction is detected and a DTC is stored. The motor control module (MCM) controls the IMA motor output based on the phase motor current sensor signals. When the W phase motor current sensor output voltage is a specified voltage for a set time, a malfunction is detected and a DTC is stored. The motor power inverter (MPI) module transmits failure information to the motor control module (MCM) via serial communication when a nonvolatile memory failure, an internal temperature sensor failure, or insulated gate bipolar transistor (IGBT) gate over voltage occurs. When the MCM receives failure information for a specified duration, a malfunction is detected and a DTC is stored. When the DC-DC converter is operating normally, the 12 V battery voltage and the output voltage read by the motor control module (MCM) are within a specified value. When the DC-DC converter malfunctions, the 12 V battery voltage drops. When the DC-DC converter output voltage value commanded by the MCM and the 12 V battery voltage value read by the MCM are compared, and a specified voltage difference condition continues for a set time, or when the MCM receives the internal malfunction information from the DC-DC converter, a DC-DC converter malfunction is detected and a DTC is stored. The motor control module (MCM) controls motor output based on the phase motor current sensor signals. When the phase motor current sensor breaks down and the output voltage drifts, there is a possibility that the motor output torque cannot be controlled properly. When the output voltage of the V phase motor current sensor is out of a specified value when the sensor input current is 0 A, the MCM detects a malfunction of the phase motor current sensor and stores a DTC. The motor control module (MCM) controls motor output based on the phase motor current sensor signals. When the phase motor current sensor breaks down and the output voltage drifts, there is a possibility that the motor output torque cannot be controlled properly. When the output voltage of the W phase motor current sensor is out of a specified value when the sensor input current is 0 A, the MCM detects a malfunction of the phase motor current sensor and stores a DTC. The motor control module (MCM) controls motor output based on the phase motor current sensor signals. When the phase motor current sensor breaks down and the output voltage drifts, there is a possibility that the motor output torque cannot be controlled properly. When the output voltage of the U phase motor current sensor is out of a specified value when the sensor input current is 0 A, the MCM detects a malfunction of the phase motor current sensor and stores a DTC. The motor control module (MCM) controls the IMA motor output based on the signal from the phase motor current sensors. When the motor current sensors malfunction, the output torque of the IMA motor may not be able to be controlled normally. The MCM monitors the sum of the three-phase current (IUPH, IVPH, and IWPH) sensors at regular intervals, and when it deviates from the permissible range (-50 A to +50 A), 10 is added to the counter. Conversely, when it is in the permissible range, 1 is subtracted from the counter. When the counter exceeds a specified amount, a malfunction of the phase motor current sensor is detected and a DTC is stored.
detection_condition: The battery module temperature sensor 3 output voltage is 0.05 V or less for at least 2.0 seconds.
Diagnosis Details
Conditions for setting the DTC
When a malfunction is detected during the first drive cycle, a Pending DTC is stored in the MCM memory. If the malfunction returns in the next
(second) drive cycle, the MIL comes on and a Confirmed DTC and the freeze data are stored.
Conditions for clearing the DTC
The MIL is cleared if the malfunction does not return in three consecutive trips in which the diagnostic runs. The MIL, the Pending DTC, the Confirmed
DTC, and the freeze data can be cleared with the scan tool Clear command or by disconnecting the battery.
Advanced Diagnostics

steps.instruction: Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).
Clear the DTC with the HDS.

Check the IMA BATTERY MODULE TEMPERATURE SENSOR 3 VOLTAGE (IMA BATTERY MODULE TEMPERATURE SENSOR 3) in the DATA LIST with the HDS. Is 4.95 V or more (-40 °F (-40 °C) or less) indicated? Go to step 4. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the battery module and the MCM.
Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Are DTC P0A9E, P0AC8, and P0ACD indicated at the same time? Go to step 20. Go to step 5.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Turn the battery module switch OFF.
Remove the IPU cover  See: Hybrid Drive Systems/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/IPU Cover Removal/Installation.
Disconnect the junction board 12P connector (A).
Connect junction board 12P connector terminal No. 10 to body ground with a jumper wire.
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).

Check the IMA BATTERY MODULE TEMPERATURE SENSOR 3 VOLTAGE (IMA BATTERY MODULE TEMPERATURE SENSOR 3) in the DATA LIST with the HDS. Is 4.95 V or more (-40 °F (-40 °C) or less) indicated? Go to step 15. Go to step 12.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Disconnect the jumper wire from the junction board 12P connector.

Check for continuity between junction board 12P connector terminals No. 9 and No. 10. Is there continuity? Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the battery module and the MCM, then go to step 32. Replace the battery module  See: Hybrid Drive Systems/Battery System, Hybrid Drive/Battery, Hybrid Drive/Service and Repair, then go to step 32.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Disconnect the jumper wire from the junction board 12P connector.
Remove the PCU lid, the PCU busplate, and the PCU cover  See: Hybrid Drive Systems/Power Control System/Drive Motor Control Module/Service and Repair.
Disconnect MCM connector B (24P).

Check for continuity between MCM connector terminal B20 and junction board 12P connector terminal No. 10. Is there continuity? Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the battery module and the MCM, then go to step 32. Repair an open in the wire between the junction board 12P connector and the MCM (B20), then go to step 32.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Turn the battery module switch OFF.
Remove the IPU cover  See: Hybrid Drive Systems/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/IPU Cover Removal/Installation.
Disconnect the junction board 12P connector (A).
Connect junction board 12P connector terminal No. 9 to body ground with a jumper wire.
Reconnect the junction board 12P connector.
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).

Check the IMA BATTERY MODULE TEMPERATURE SENSOR 1 VOLTAGE (IMA BATTERY MODULE TEMPERATURE SENSOR 1), the IMA BATTERY MODULE TEMPERATURE SENSOR 2 VOLTAGE (IMA BATTERY MODULE TEMPERATURE SENSOR 2), and the IMA BATTERY MODULE TEMPERATURE SENSOR 3 VOLTAGE (IMA BATTERY MODULE TEMPERATURE SENSOR 3) in the DATA LIST with the HDS. Do all sensors indicate 4.95 V or more (-40 °F(-40 °C) or less)? Replace the battery module  See: Hybrid Drive Systems/Battery System, Hybrid Drive/Battery, Hybrid Drive/Service and Repair, then go to step 32. Go to step 28.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Remove the PCU lid, the PCU busplate, and the PCU cover  See: Hybrid Drive Systems/Power Control System/Drive Motor Control Module/Service and Repair.
Disconnect MCM connector B (24P).

Check for continuity between MCM connector terminal B22 and junction board 12P connector terminal No. 9. Is there continuity? Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the junction board 12P connector and the MCM, then go to step 32. Repair an open in the wire between the junction board 12P connector and the MCM (B22), then go to step 32.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Reconnect all connectors.
Reinstall the PCU cover, the PCU busplate, and the PCU lid.
Reinstall the IPU cover  See: Hybrid Drive Systems/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/IPU Cover Removal/Installation, then turn the battery module switch ON.
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).
Clear the DTC with the HDS.

Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P0ACD indicated? Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the battery module and the MCM, then go to step 1. is complete. If any other Pending or Confirmed DTCs are indicated, go to the indicated DTC's troubleshooting. Advanced Diagnostics Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P0AC0 (65): Battery Current Sensor 1 Circuit Malfunction P0AC0 (65) DTC P0AC0: Battery Current Sensor 1 Circuit Malfunction
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).
Clear the DTC with the HDS.

Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P0AC1, P0AC2, P0B10, or P0B11 indicated? Do the troubleshooting for DTC P0AC1  See: P0AC1, P0AC2  See: P0AC2, P0B10  See: P0B10, or P0B11  See: P0B11. Diagnosis Details Conditions for setting the DTC When a malfunction is detected, the MIL comes on and a Pending DTC, a Confirmed DTC, and the freeze data are stored in the MCM memory. Conditions for clearing the DTC The MIL is cleared if the malfunction does not return in three consecutive trips in which the diagnostic runs. The MIL, the Pending DTC, the Confirmed DTC, and the freeze data can be cleared with the scan tool Clear command or by disconnecting the battery. Start the engine.

Drive the vehicle with the accessories on (blower fan MAX, headlights ON, etc.). Without turning the ignition switch to LOCK (0), accelerate and decelerate repeatedly under the condition to use IMA assist until the IMA battery level indicator shows one segment. - Drive the vehicle in this manner only if the traffic regulations and ambient conditions allow. Diagnosis Details Conditions for setting the DTC When a malfunction is detected during the first drive cycle, a Pending DTC is stored in the MCM memory. If the malfunction returns in the next (second) drive cycle, the MIL comes on and the Confirmed DTC and the freeze data are stored. Conditions for clearing the DTC The MIL is cleared if the malfunction does not return in three consecutive trips in which the diagnostic runs. The MIL, the Pending DTC, the Confirmed DTC, and the freeze data can be cleared with the scan tool Clear command or by disconnecting the battery. P0A7F (78) DTC P0A7F: Battery Module Deterioration

Diagnosis Details Conditions for setting the DTC When a malfunction is detected, a Pending DTC, a Confirmed DTC, and the freeze data are stored in the MCM memory. The MIL does not come on. Conditions for clearing the DTC The Pending DTC, the Confirmed DTC, and the freeze data can be cleared with the scan tool Clear command or by disconnecting the battery. P0A94 (48) DTC P0A94: DC-DC Converter Output Low Voltage
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).
Clear the DTC with the HDS.
Start the engine, and let it idle for 2 minutes under these conditions: - Headlights on high beam - Blower fan at maximum speed - Rear window defogger on

Monitor the OBD STATUS for P0A94 in the DTCs MENU with the HDS. Does the HDS indicate FAILED? Go to step 5. If the HDS indicates PASSED, intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for poor connections, loose terminals, or damaged insulation at the DC-DC converter (+B BACK UP line), the +B terminal (on the battery terminal fuse box), the MCM (+B BACK UP line), the No. B1 BACK UP (15A) fuse in the under-dash fuse/relay box, and DC-DC converter ground cable (G4). Reconnect all connectors.

dtcs.description: Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P0B0F (113): Battery Current Sensor 2 Circuit Malfunction The motor control module (MCM) calculates the offset value of the battery current sensor during a no voltage condition of the battery module immediately after the ignition switch is turned to ON (II). The MCM converts (analog/digital conversion) the ISOCF signal input voltage and when the difference between the maximum value and the minimum value of eight continuous inputs are within 0.015 V, the average value of eight battery current sensor inputs are calculated. When the battery current sensor average value is out of a specified range, a malfunction is detected and a DTC is stored. When the calculated current difference between ISOC signal and ISOCF signal deviates from a specified range for a set time, a malfunction is detected and a DTC is stored. P0B10 (117) DTC P0B10: Battery Current Sensor 2 Circuit Low Voltage The motor control module (MCM) controls charge/discharge of the battery module based on the battery current sensor signal. When the battery current sensor ISOCF signal voltage is a specified voltage for a set time, the MCM detects a malfunction and stores a DTC. The motor control module (MCM) controls charge/discharge of the battery module based on the battery current sensor signal. When the battery current sensor ISOCF signal voltage is a specified voltage for a set time, the MCM detects a malfunction and stores a DTC. The air/fuel ratio (A/F) sensor (sensor 1) has a linear signal output in relation to the oxygen concentration. The engine control module (ECM)/powertrain control module (PCM) computes the air/fuel ratio from the A/F sensor output voltage and uses fuel feedback control to improve exhaust emissions. The ECM/PCM monitors the A/F sensor output voltage during deceleration with the throttle fully closed, and if the output voltage deviates greatly from normal oxygen concentration levels, it detects a malfunction and stores a DTC. The motor control module (MCM) applies 5 V to the battery current sensor and monitors the voltage. When the voltage is a specified voltage for a set time, a malfunction is detected and a DTC is stored. The motor control module (MCM) controls the IMA motor by detecting the rotor position angle using the motor rotor position sensor. The MCM detects zero point offset to improve torque accuracy and motor efficiency. When the zero point offset detection value is out of a permissible range, the MCM detects a malfunction and stores a DTC. The motor control module (MCM) detects excessive phase current of the IMA motor. When the current is excessive, the MCM detects a malfunction and stores a DTC.
detection_condition: One of these symptoms occurs:
Symptom 1
The average value of the battery current sensor input deviates from the range of 2.24 V to 2.76 V.
Symptom 2
The absolute current value difference between the ISOC signal and the ISOCF signal is 10 A or more for at least 2 seconds when the battery current
sensor current value is in the range of -30 A to 30 A.
Diagnosis Details
Conditions for setting the DTC
When a malfunction is detected, the MIL comes on and a Pending DTC, a Confirmed DTC, and the freeze data are stored in the MCM memory.
Conditions for clearing the DTC
The MIL is cleared if the malfunction does not return in three consecutive trips in which the diagnostic runs. The MIL, the Pending DTC, the Confirmed
DTC, and the freeze data can be cleared with the scan tool Clear command or by disconnecting the battery.
The battery current sensor ISOCF signal voltage is 0.05 V or less for at least 0.5 second.
Diagnosis Details
Conditions for setting the DTC
When a malfunction is detected, the MIL comes on and a Pending DTC, a Confirmed DTC, and the freeze data are stored in the MCM memory.
Conditions for clearing the DTC
The MIL is cleared if the malfunction does not return in three consecutive trips in which the diagnostic runs. The MIL, the Pending DTC, the Confirmed
DTC, and the freeze data can be cleared with the scan tool Clear command or by disconnecting the battery.
The battery current sensor ISOCF signal voltage is 4.95 V or more for at least 0.5 second.
Diagnosis Details
Conditions for setting the DTC

Conditions for clearing the DTC

DTC, and the freeze data can be cleared with the scan tool Clear command or by disconnecting the battery.
Advanced Diagnostics
Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P2A00: Air/Fuel Ratio (A/F) Sensor (Sensor 1) Range/Performance Problem
The A/F sensor (sensor 1) output voltage is 2.8 V or less, or 4.8 V or more.
The battery current sensor voltage is 4.8 V or less for at least 2 seconds.
Diagnosis Details
Conditions for setting the DTC

DTC, and the freeze data can be cleared with the scan tool Clear command or by disconnecting the battery.
Advanced Diagnostics
Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P15AA (93): Motor Rotor Position Not Learned
The motor rotor position sensor angle detection value is -25° or less, or +25° or more.
An over current detection signal is detected at least three times within 3 seconds.

Start the engine. Hold the engine speed at 3,000 rpm without load (CVT in P or N, M/T in neutral) until the radiator fan comes on.
Drive the vehicle at a speed between 25 - 55 mph (40 - 88 km/h) for at least 5 minutes.
Drive immediately at a steady speed between 55 - 75 mph (88 - 120 km/h) for at least 10 seconds.

Decelerate with the throttle valve fully closed for at least 5 seconds. - If the EVAP monitor runs instead of the HO2S monitor, turn the engine off, then restart it, and the HO2S monitor will restart. - Drive the vehicle in this manner only if the traffic regulations and ambient conditions allow. Diagnosis Details Conditions for setting the DTC When a malfunction is detected during the first drive cycle, a Pending DTC is stored in the ECM/PCM memory. If the malfunction returns in the next (second) drive cycle, the MIL comes on and a Confirmed DTC and the freeze data are stored. Conditions for clearing the DTC The MIL is cleared if the malfunction does not return in three consecutive trips in which the diagnostic runs. The MIL, the Pending DTC, the Confirmed DTC, and the freeze data can be cleared with the scan tool Clear command or by disconnecting the battery. P2A00, P2A00 DTC P2A00: A/F Sensor (Sensor 1) Range/Performance Problem
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).
Clear the DTC with the HDS.
Start the engine. Hold the engine speed at 3,000 rpm without load (CVT in P or N, M/T in neutral) until the radiator fan comes on, then let it idle.

Test-drive under these conditions: - Engine coolant temperature (ECT SENSOR 1) above 158 °F (70 °C) - CVT in D, M/T in 3rd or 4th - Vehicle speed between 25-55 mph (40-88 km/h) for 5 minutes - Drive at a steady speed between 55-75 mph (88-120 km/h) for 10 seconds, then decelerate (with the throttle fully closed) for 5 seconds

Monitor the OBD STATUS for DTC P2A00 in the DTCs MENU with the HDS. Does the HDS indicate FAILED? Go to step 6. If the HDS indicates PASSED, intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the A/F sensor (Sensor 1) and the ECM/PCM. If the HDS indicates EXECUTING, keep driving until a result comes on. If the HDS indicates OUT OF CONDITION, go to step 3 and recheck.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Replace the A/F sensor (Sensor 1)  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Oxygen Sensor/Service and Repair/A/F Sensor Replacement.
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).
Reset the ECM/PCM with the HDS.
Do the ECM/PCM idle learn procedure  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/ECM/PCM Idle Learn Procedure.

Test-drive under these conditions: - Engine coolant temperature (ECT SENSOR 1) above 158 °F (70 °C) - CVT in D, M/T in 3rd or 4th - Vehicle speed between 25-55 mph (40-88 km/h) for 5 minutes - Drive at a steady speed between 55-75 mph(88-120 km/h) for 10 seconds, then decelerate (with the throttle fully closed) for 5 seconds
Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P2A00 indicated? Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the A/F sensor (Sensor 1) and the ECM/PCM, then go to step 1. Go to step 13.

Monitor the OBD STATUS for DTC P2A00 in the DTCs MENU with the HDS. Does the HDS indicate PASSED? is complete. If any other Pending or Confirmed DTCs were indicated in step 12, go to the indicated DTC's troubleshooting. If the HDS indicates FAILED, check for poor connections or loose terminals at the A/F sensor (Sensor 1) and the ECM/PCM, then go to step 1. If the HDS indicates EXECUTING, keep driving until a result comes on. If the HDS indicates OUT OF CONDITION, go to step 11. P06B1 (79) DTC P06B1: IMA Battery Current Sensor Circuit Malfunction

Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P06B1 indicated? Reconnect all connectors.

Reconnect the four IMA motor power cables to the phase motor current sensor.
Reinstall the MCM  See: Hybrid Drive Systems/Power Control System/Drive Motor Control Module/Service and Repair.
Reinstall the PCU cover, the PCU busplate, and the PCU lid  See: Hybrid Drive Systems/Power Control System/Drive Motor Control Module/Service and Repair.

Update the MCM if it does not have the latest software  See: Hybrid Drive Systems/Power Control System/Drive Motor Control Module/Testing and Inspection, or substitute a known-good MCM.
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).

Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P06B1 indicated? Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the MCM, the phase motor current sensor, and the battery current sensor. If the MCM was updated, substitute a known-good MCM, then recheck. If the MCM was substituted, go to step 1. If the MCM was updated, troubleshooting is complete. If the MCM was substituted, replace the original MCM  See: Hybrid Drive Systems/Power Control System/Drive Motor Control Module/Service and Repair. If any other Pending or Confirmed DTCs were indicated, go to the indicated DTC's . Advanced Diagnostics Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P06B1 (79): IMA Battery Current Sensor Circuit Malfunction
Reset the MCM. (Disconnect the 12 V battery terminal once and reconnect it.)

Start the engine, and wait for at least 1 minute without pressing the accelerator pedal. Diagnosis Details Conditions for setting the DTC When a malfunction is detected during the first drive cycle, a Pending DTC is stored in the MCM memory. If the malfunction returns in the next (second) drive cycle, a Confirmed DTC and the freeze data are stored. The MIL does not come on. Conditions for clearing the DTC The Pending DTC, the Confirmed DTC, and the freeze data can be cleared with the scan tool Clear command or by disconnecting the battery. P15AA (93) DTC P15AA: Motor Rotor Position Not Learned
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).
Clear the DTC with the HDS.
Start the engine, and let it idle 1 minute.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), then turn it to ON (II).
Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Are any DTC(s) except P15AA indicated? Go to the indicated DTC's troubleshooting. Go to step 6.
Clear the DTC with the HDS.
Start the engine, and hold the engine speed between 3,500 rpm and 4,000 rpm without load (in P or N (CVT model)) (in neutral (M/T model)) until the SOC increases to above 70%.

Do the MOTOR ROTOR POSITION CALIBRATION in the IMA ADJUSTMENT MENU with the HDS. Does the HDS indicate COMPLETE? is complete. Check the installation of the IMA motor rotor position sensor and the MCM. If needed, reinstall the parts, then go to step 9.
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).
Clear the DTC with the HDS.
Start the engine, and let it idle 1 minute.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), then turn it to ON (II).

Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P15AA indicated? Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the IMA motor rotor position sensor and the MCM, then go to step 1. Go to step 14.

Monitor the OBD STATUS for P15AA in the DTCs MENU with the HDS. Does the HDS indicate PASSED? is complete. If any other Pending or Confirmed DTCs were indicated in step 13, go to the indicated DTC's troubleshooting. If the HDS indicates FAILED, check for poor connections or loose terminals at the motor rotor position sensor and the MCM, then go to step 1. If the HDS indicates NOT COMPLETED, go to step 11. Advanced Diagnostics Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P15A5 (85): Motor Current Sensor Circuit Malfunction P15A5 (85) DTC P15A5: Motor Current Sensor Circuit Malfunction
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).
Clear the DTC with the HDS.
Start the engine, and let it idle 1 minute under these conditions: - Headlights on high beam - Blower fan at maximum speed - Rear window defogger on

Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P0A3F, P0A5E, P0A5F, P0A61, P0A62, P0A64, P0A65, P0BE6, P0BEA, P0BEE, P0BFD, P1437, and/or P1440 indicated? Go to the indicated DTC's troubleshooting. Go to step 5.
Test-drive the vehicle using wide open throttle for at least 2 seconds, then decelerate while lightly braking.

At idle, individually check the amperage of the U PHASE MOTOR CURRENT SENSOR, the V PHASE MOTOR CURRENT SENSOR, and the W PHASE MOTOR CURRENT SENSOR in the DATA LIST with the HDS. Do the values vary from 0 A? Replace the phase motor current sensor  See: Hybrid Drive Systems/Power Control System/Voltage Inverter Module/Service and Repair, then go to step 28. Go to step 8.

Turn the battery module switch OFF.
Remove the IPU cover  See: Hybrid Drive Systems/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/IPU Cover Removal/Installation.
Remove the PCU lid, the PCU busplate, and the PCU cover  See: Hybrid Drive Systems/Power Control System/Drive Motor Control Module/Service and Repair.
Disconnect MCM connector C (22P).
Disconnect the MPI module 12P connector (A).

Check for continuity between these terminals: - MCM connector terminal C8 and MPI module 12P connector terminal No. 11. - MCM connector terminal C16 and MPI module 12P connector terminal No. 5. - MCM connector terminal C17 and MPI module 12P connector terminal No. 10. - MCM connector terminal C1 and MPI module 12P connector terminal No. 4. - MCM connector terminal C9 and MPI module 12P connector terminal No. 9. - MCM connector terminal C2 and MPI module 12P connector terminal No. 3. Is there continuity between all of the terminals? Go to step 15. Repair an open in the wire between the MPI module 12P connector and the MCM (C1, C2, C8, C9, C16, C17), then go to step 28.

Check for continuity between these MCM connector C (22P) terminals and body ground individually: - C1 and body ground - C2 and body ground - C8 and body ground - C9 and body ground - C16 and body ground - C17 and body ground Is there continuity between each terminal and body ground? Repair a short in the wire between the MPI module 12P connector and the MCM (C1, C2, C8, C9, C16, C17), then go to step 28. Go to step 16.
Remove the IMA motor power cables (A) from the phase motor current sensor.

Replace the IMA motor power cable  See: Hybrid Drive Systems/Drive Motor/Generator/Drive Motor Power Cable/Service and Repair/IMA Motor Power Cable Removal/Installation, then go to step 28. Go to step 19.
Substitute a known-good MPI module.

dtcs.description: , P15C1 DTC P15C1: Brake Booster Pressure Sensor B Circuit High Voltage

steps.instruction: Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).

Check BRAKE BOOSTER PRESSURE SENSOR B in the EVPS DATA LIST with the HDS. Is there 4.5 V or more? Go to step 3. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for poor connections or loose terminals at brake booster pressure sensor B and the ECM/PCM.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Disconnect brake booster pressure sensor B 3P connector.
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).
Measure the voltage between brake booster pressure sensor B 3P connector terminals No. 1 and No. 2. Is there 4.8 V or more? Go to step 7. Go to step 20.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Short the SCS line with the HDS.
Disconnect ECM/PCM connector B (44P).
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).

Measure the voltage between brake booster pressure sensor B 3P connector terminals No. 2 and body ground. Is there 0.5 V or more? Repair a short to power in the wire between the ECM/PCM and brake booster pressure sensor B. Go to step 12.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).

Substitute a known-good brake booster pressure sensor B  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Power Brake Assist/Brake Booster Vacuum Sensor/Service and Repair/Brake Booster Pressure Sensor B Replacement.
Reconnect all connectors.
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).
Clear the DTC with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).

Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P15C1 indicated? Update the ECM/PCM if it does not have the latest software  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/ECM/PCM Update, or substitute a known-good ECM/PCM, then recheck. If the symptom/indication goes away with a known-good ECM/PCM, replace the original ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair. Replace the original brake booster pressure sensor B  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Power Brake Assist/Brake Booster Vacuum Sensor/Service and Repair/Brake Booster Pressure Sensor B Replacement.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Connect brake booster pressure sensor B 3P connector terminals No. 2 to body ground with a jumper wire.
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).
Check BRAKE BOOSTER PRESSURE SENSOR B in the EVPS DATA LIST with the HDS. Is there 0.1 V or less? Go to step 24. Go to step 32.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Connect brake booster pressure sensor B 3P connector terminals No. 1 and No. 2 with a jumper wire.
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).

Check BRAKE BOOSTER PRESSURE SENSOR B in the EVPS DATA LIST with the HDS. Is there 0.1 V or less? Replace brake booster pressure sensor B  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Power Brake Assist/Brake Booster Vacuum Sensor/Service and Repair/Brake Booster Pressure Sensor B Replacement. Go to step 28.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Short the SCS line with the HDS.
Disconnect ECM/PCM connector A (44P).

Check for continuity between ECM/PCM connector A (44P) terminal No. 9 and brake booster pressure sensor B 3P connector terminal No. 1. Is there continuity? Update the ECM/PCM if it does not have the latest software  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/ECM/PCM Update, or substitute a known-good ECM/PCM, then recheck. If the symptom/indication goes away with a known-good ECM/PCM, replace the original ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair. Repair an open in the wire between the ECM/PCM and brake booster pressure sensor B.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Short the SCS line with the HDS.
Disconnect ECM/PCM connector B (44P).

Check for continuity between ECM/PCM connector B (44P) terminal No.4 and brake booster pressure sensor B 3P connector terminal No. 2. Is there continuity? Update the ECM/PCM if it does not have the latest software  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/ECM/PCM Update, or substitute a known-good ECM/PCM, then recheck. If the symptom/indication goes away with a known-good ECM/PCM, replace the original ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair. Repair an open in the wire between the ECM/PCM and brake booster pressure sensor B.

dtcs.description: , P15C3 DTC P15C3: Brake Booster Pressure Sensor A/B Voltage Incorrect Correlation Special Tools Required - Vacuum/Pressure Gauge, 0-4 inHg 07JAZ-001000B

steps.instruction: Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).
Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs in the PGM-FI system with the HDS. Are there DTC(s) indicated in the PGM-FI system? Go to the indicated DTC's troubleshooting. Go to step 3.
Clear the DTC with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).

Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P15C3 indicated? Go to step 7. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for poor connections or loose terminals at brake booster pressure sensors and the ECM/PCM.
Start the engine. Hold the engine speed at 3,000 rpm without load (CVT in P or N, M/T in Neutral) until the radiator fan comes on, then let it idle.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).

Press the brake pedal several times to deplete the vacuum with monitoring BRAKE BOOSTER PRESSURE SENSOR A and BRAKE BOOSTER PRESSURE SENSOR B in the EVPS DATA LIST with the HDS. Is the difference between BRAKE BOOSTER PRESSURE SENSOR A and BRAKE BOOSTER PRESSURE SENSOR B 0.1 V or less? Repair a short in the wire between brake booster pressure sensor A and brake booster pressure sensor B. Go to step 11.

Check BRAKE BOOSTER PRESSURE SENSOR A and BRAKE BOOSTER PRESSURE SENSOR B in the EVPS DATA LIST with the HDS. Is the difference between BRAKE BOOSTER PRESSURE SENSOR A and BRAKE BOOSTER PRESSURE SENSOR B 6 kPa (50 mmHg, 1.8 inHg) or less? Go to step 14. Go to step 12.

Check BRAKE BOOSTER PRESSURE SENSOR A and MAP SENSOR in the EVPS DATA LIST with the HDS. Is the difference between BRAKE BOOSTER PRESSURE SENSOR A and MAP SENSOR 6 kPa (50 mmHg, 1.8 inHg) or less? Go to step 13. Go to step 20.

Check BRAKE BOOSTER PRESSURE SENSOR B and MAP SENSOR in the EVPS DATA LIST with the HDS. Is the difference between BRAKE BOOSTER PRESSURE SENSOR B and MAP SENSOR 6 kPa (50 mmHg, 1.8 inHg) or less? Compare value of MAP SENSOR with the value of BRAKE BOOSTER PRESSURE SENSOR A and BRAKE BOOSTER PRESSURE SENSOR B, then check the sensor which has a larger difference in the value. - Brake booster pressure sensor A: Go to step 20. - Brake booster pressure sensor B: Go to step 23. Go to step 23.
Press the brake pedal several times to deplete the vacuum.
Start the engine. Hold the engine speed at 3,000 rpm without load (CVT in P or N, M/T in Neutral) until the radiator fan comes on, then let it idle.
With light pedal force, press the brake pedal several times slowly.

Check BRAKE BOOSTER PRESSURE SENSOR A and BRAKE BOOSTER PRESSURE B in the EVPS DATA LIST with the HDS. Is the difference between BRAKE BOOSTER PRESSURE SENSOR A and BRAKE BOOSTER PRESSURE SENSOR B 6 kPa(50 mmHg, 1.8 inHg) or less? Go to step 26. Go to step 18.

Check BRAKE BOOSTER PRESSURE SENSOR A and MAP SENSOR in the EVPS DATA LIST with the HDS. Is the difference between BRAKE BOOSTER PRESSURE SENSOR A and MAP SENSOR 6 kPa(50 mmHg, 1.8 inHg) or less? Go to step 19. Go to step 20.

Check BRAKE BOOSTER PRESSURE SENSOR B and MAP SENSOR in the EVPS DATA LIST with the HDS. Is the difference between BRAKE BOOSTER PRESSURE SENSOR B and MAP SENSOR 6 kPa(50 mmHg, 1.8 inHg) or less? Compare value of MAP SENSOR with the value of BRAKE BOOSTER PRESSURE SENSOR A and BRAKE BOOSTER PRESSURE SENSOR B, then check the sensor which has a larger difference in the value. - BRAKE BOOSTER PRESSURE SENSOR A: Go to step 20. - BRAKE BOOSTER PRESSURE SENSOR B: Go to step 23. Go to step 23.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Disconnect the vacuum hose from brake booster pressure sensor A.

Start the engine, then check for vacuum at the vacuum hose. Is there vacuum? Replace brake booster pressure sensor A  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Power Brake Assist/Brake Booster Vacuum Sensor/Service and Repair/Brake Booster Pressure Sensor A Replacement. Repair the clogged hose which connects brake booster pressure sensor A to the brake vacuum hose.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Disconnect the vacuum hose from brake booster pressure sensor B.

Start the engine, then check for vacuum at the vacuum hose. Is there vacuum? Replace brake booster pressure sensor B  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Power Brake Assist/Brake Booster Vacuum Sensor/Service and Repair/Brake Booster Pressure Sensor B Replacement. Repair the clogged hose which connects brake booster pressure sensor B to the brake vacuum hose.
Clear the DTC with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).
Press the brake pedal several times to deplete the vacuum.
Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P15C3 indicated? Go to step 31. Check for poor connections or loose terminals at brake booster pressure sensor A, brake booster pressure sensor B, and the ECM/PCM.
Check BRAKE BOOSTER PRESSURE SENSOR A and BRAKE BOOSTER PRESSURE B in the EVPS DATA LIST with the HDS.
Install the vacuum/pressure gauge (A) and a suitable hose (B) between the brake booster and the vacuum hose (C).
Start the engine. Hold the engine speed at 3,000 rpm without load (CVT in P or N, M/T in Neutral) until the radiator fan comes on, then let it idle.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).
Set the value of BRAKE BOOSTER PRESSURE SENSOR A and BRAKE BOOSTER PRESSURE SENSOR B to the values in the freeze data with pressing the brake pedal several times with low treading strength.

Check BRAKE BOOSTER PRESSURE SENSOR A in the EVPS DATA LIST with the HDS. Is the difference between BRAKE BOOSTER PRESSURE SENSOR A and the value of vacuum/pressure gauge 6 kPa(50 mmHg, 1.8 inHg) or less? Go to step 38. Replace brake booster pressure sensor A  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Power Brake Assist/Brake Booster Vacuum Sensor/Service and Repair/Brake Booster Pressure Sensor A Replacement.

Check BRAKE BOOSTER PRESSURE SENSOR B in the EVPS DATA LIST with the HDS. Is the difference between BRAKE BOOSTER PRESSURE SENSOR B and the value of vacuum/pressure gauge 6 kPa(50 mmHg, 1.8 inHg) or less? Compare the value of the vacuum/pressure gauge with the value of BRAKE BOOSTER PRESSURE SENSOR A and BRAKE BOOSTER PRESSURE SENSOR B in the recorded data in the freeze data. Do this process a few times, then replace brake booster pressure sensor A  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Power Brake Assist/Brake Booster Vacuum Sensor/Service and Repair/Brake Booster Pressure Sensor A Replacement or brake booster pressure sensor B  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Power Brake Assist/Brake Booster Vacuum Sensor/Service and Repair/Brake Booster Pressure Sensor B Replacement, which ever has the larger difference in the value. Replace brake booster pressure sensor B  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Power Brake Assist/Brake Booster Vacuum Sensor/Service and Repair/Brake Booster Pressure Sensor B Replacement.

dtcs.description: Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P15C4 (118): Auto Idle Stop System Malfunction There are several system or units (for example the A/C system) where if they detect a malfunction, can over-ride the auto idle stop and keep the engine running. If one of these systems or units over-rides the auto idle stop, the corresponding warning indicator comes on. Not every unit or system that can over-ride the auto idle stop has its own indicator. In these cases, the system or unit sends signal to the engine control module (ECM)/powertrain control module (PCM), which sends a signal to the motor control module (MCM) to turn on the IMA system indicator and stores a DTC. P15C4 (118) DTC P15C4: Auto Idle Stop System Malfunction (CVT model) The control program in the powertrain control module (PCM) has a rewriting function that can be done in the field. The programs in the PCM CPU are the powertrain control program (rewritable program) and the update program (non-rewritable program). When the program rewriting process is not finished normally, such as turning off the PCM power during the rewriting process, and then turning the ignition switch to ON (II) again, the CVT CPU starts according to the rewriting function program, and this DTC is sent to the FI CPU via serial communication and stored in the PCM. Temperature abnormalities are detected by the microcomputer in the DC-DC converter, and this information is sent to the motor control module (MCM) via serial communication. When the abnormal temperature sensor information continues for a set time, the MCM detects a malfunction and stores a DTC. The controller area network (CAN) transmits/receives pulsing signals to/from the control modules simultaneously by using two signal lines (F-CAN H and F-CAN L). When the information is not sent from the CVT CPU via the F-CAN lines and this condition continues for a specified time or when the information sent from the CVT CPU is abnormal and this condition continues for a specified cycle, the powertrain control module (PCM) detects a malfunction and stores a DTC. The integrated motor assist-controller area network (IMA-CAN) uses two signal lines, IMA-CAN H and IMA-CAN L, and it receives and transmits multiple signals simultaneously to/from multiple control modules. When the information is not sent from the CVT CPU via the IMA-CAN lines and this condition continues for a specified time or when the information sent from the CVT CPU is abnormal and this condition continues for a specified cycle, the powertrain control module (PCM) detects a malfunction and stores a DTC. The controller area network (CAN) transmits/receives pulsing signals to/from the control modules simultaneously by using two signal lines (F-CAN H and F-CAN L). When F-CAN communication between the FI CPU and the CVT CPU inside the powertrain control module (PCM) is abnormal and F-CAN communication is interrupted for a specified time, the PCM detects a malfunction and stores a DTC. The integrated motor assist-controller area network (IMA-CAN) uses two signal lines, IMA-CAN H and IMA-CAN L, and it receives and transmits multiple signals simultaneously to/from multiple control modules. When IMA-CAN communication between the FI CPU and the CVT CPU inside the powertrain control module (PCM) is abnormal and IMA-CAN communication is interrupted for a specified time, the PCM detects a malfunction and stores a DTC. The quick warm-up system supplies additional air and retards the ignition timing when the engine is cold to activate the catalytic converter as quickly as possible. When the actual amount of air is less than the target amount, the engine control module (ECM)/powertrain control module (PCM) detects a malfunction and stores a DTC. The quick warm-up system supplies additional air and retards the ignition timing when the engine is cold to activate the catalytic converter as quickly as possible. When the actual engine speed is a specified fast idle value, and it continues for a specified time, the engine control module (ECM)/powertrain control module (PCM) detects a malfunction and stores a DTC. When a malfunction occurs in the internal communication between the FI CPU and the CVT CPU in the powertrain control module (PCM) and the internal communication is interrupted for a set time, or when the watchdog timer that monitors the CVT CPU detects an abnormality in the CVT CPU and notifies the FI CPU via the CVT CPU check signal line, the PCM detects a malfunction and stores a DTC. The engine control module (ECM)/powertrain control module (PCM) is equipped with a keep alive memory (KAM). The data (control learn data, etc.) for powertrain control and information (vehicle identification number (VIN), etc.) related to the vehicle control is stored in the KAM, so that it can be maintained even when power is not supplied to the ECM/PCM, such as when the battery is disconnected. When power is restored to the ECM/PCM, the CPU retrieves the stored information from the KAM, but when the data retrieval process is not finished normally, the ECM/PCM detects a malfunction and stores a DTC. The CPU writes data to the KAM from the CPU: Control related data is written when the ignition switch is turned to ON (II), and vehicle information when commanded from the HDS. If the data writing process is not completed normally, the ECM/PCM detects a malfunction and stores a DTC. The powertrain control module (PCM) is equipped with a keep alive memory (KAM). The data (control learn data, etc.) for powertrain control and information (vehicle identification number (VIN), etc.) related to vehicle control is stored in the KAM, so that it can be maintained even when power is not supplied to the PCM, such as when the battery is disconnected. When power is restored to the PCM, the CPU retrieves the stored information from the KAM, but when the data retrieval process is not finished normally, the PCM detects a malfunction and stores a DTC. The CPU writes data to the KAM from the CPU: Control related data is written when the ignition switch is turned to ON (II), and vehicle information when commanded from the HDS. If the data writing process is not completed normally, the PCM detects a malfunction and stores a DTC.
detection_condition: The MCM receives a failure related to the auto idle stop prohibition for at least 2 seconds.
Diagnosis Details
Conditions for setting the DTC
When a malfunction is detected during the first drive cycle, a Pending DTC is stored in the MCM memory. If the malfunction returns in the next
(second) drive cycle, a Confirmed DTC and the freeze data are stored. The MIL does not come on.
Conditions for clearing the DTC
The Pending DTC, the Confirmed DTC, and the freeze data can be cleared with the scan tool Clear command or by disconnecting the battery.
With CVT
The program rewriting process does not finish normally due to an irregular process, such as turning off the PCM power during the CVT CPU rewriting
process, and the ignition switch is turned to ON (II) again.
Diagnosis Details
Conditions for setting the DTC
When a malfunction is detected, the MIL comes on and a Pending DTC, a Confirmed DTC, and the freeze data are stored in the PCM memory.
Conditions for clearing the DTC
The MIL is cleared if the malfunction does not return in three consecutive trips in which the diagnostic runs. The MIL, the Pending DTC, the Confirmed
DTC, and the freeze data can be cleared with the scan tool Clear command or by disconnecting the battery.
The abnormal temperature sensor information continues for at least 2 seconds.
Diagnosis Details
Conditions for setting the DTC
When a malfunction is detected, a Pending DTC, a Confirmed DTC, and the freeze data are stored in the MCM memory. The MIL does not come on.
Conditions for clearing the DTC
The Pending DTC, the Confirmed DTC, and the freeze data can be cleared with the scan tool Clear command or by disconnecting the battery.
One of these conditions is met:
The PCM does not receive any signals via the F-CAN lines for at least 1 second.
The information sent from the CVT CPU is abnormal at least 20 times.
Diagnosis Details
Conditions for setting the DTC
When a malfunction is detected, the MIL comes on and a Pending DTC, a Confirmed DTC, and the freeze data are stored in the PCM memory.
Conditions for clearing the DTC
The MIL is cleared if the malfunction does not return in three consecutive trips in which the diagnostic runs. The MIL, the Pending DTC, the Confirmed
DTC, and the freeze data can be cleared with the scan tool Clear command or by disconnecting the battery.
One of these conditions is met:
The PCM does not receive any signals via the IMA-CAN lines.
The information sent from the CVT CPU is abnormal at least 20 times.
Diagnosis Details
Conditions for setting the DTC
When a malfunction is detected, the MIL comes on and a Pending DTC, a Confirmed DTC, and the freeze data are stored in the PCM memory.
Conditions for clearing the DTC
The MIL is cleared if the malfunction does not return in three consecutive trips in which the diagnostic runs. The MIL, the Pending DTC, the Confirmed
DTC, and the freeze data can be cleared with the scan tool Clear command or by disconnecting the battery.
No signals from the FI CPU via the F-CAN are received for at least 1.5 seconds.
No signals from the FI CPU via the IMA-CAN are received for at least 1.5 seconds.
The total airflow is decreased by a factor of 0.58 for at least 10 seconds.
The engine speed is 2,400 rpm or more for at least 3.5 seconds.
One of these symptoms occur:
Symptom 1
The internal communication between the FI CPU and the CVT CPU is abnormal or the internal communication is interrupted for at least 1 second.
Symptom 2
The watchdog timer that monitors the CVT CPU detects an abnormality, and the FI CPU receives the CVT CPU check signal for at least 200
milliseconds.
Diagnosis Details
Conditions for setting the DTC
When a malfunction is detected, the MIL comes on and a Pending DTC, a Confirmed DTC, and the freeze data are stored in the PCM memory.
Conditions for clearing the DTC
The MIL is cleared if the malfunction does not return in three consecutive trips in which the diagnostic runs. The MIL, the Pending DTC, the Confirmed
DTC, and the freeze data can be cleared with the scan tool Clear command or by disconnecting the battery.
A malfunction is detected whenever the keep alive data retrieval and writing process is not completed normally.
Diagnosis Details
Conditions for setting the DTC
When a malfunction is detected, the MIL comes on and a Pending DTC, a Confirmed DTC, and the freeze data are stored in the ECM/PCM memory.
Conditions for clearing the DTC
The MIL is cleared if the malfunction does not return in three consecutive trips in which the diagnostic runs. The MIL, the Pending DTC, the Confirmed
DTC, and the freeze data can be cleared with the scan tool Clear command or by disconnecting the battery.
CVT System
Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P062F: Powertrain Control Module (PCM) Internal Control Module Error (CVT System)
A malfunction is detected whenever the keep alive data retrieval and writing process is not completed normally.
Diagnosis Details
Conditions for setting the DTC
When a malfunction is detected, the MIL comes on and a Pending DTC, a Confirmed DTC, and the freeze data are stored in the PCM memory.
Conditions for clearing the DTC
The MIL is cleared if the malfunction does not return in three consecutive trips in which the diagnostic runs. The MIL, the Pending DTC, the Confirmed
DTC, and the freeze data can be cleared with the scan tool Clear command or by disconnecting the battery.

steps.instruction: Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).
Clear the DTC with the HDS.
Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P0102 indicated? Do the troubleshooting for DTC P0102  See: P0102, then reset the PCM with the HDS. Go to step 4.

Check the AUTO IDLE STOP DOES NOT OCCUR (BRAKE) in the PGM-FI SYSTEM with the HDS. Does the HDS indicate EXIST? Go to the brake booster pressure monitoring system's general troubleshooting information  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Power Brake Assist/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview. When the brake booster pressure monitoring system troubleshooting is completed, clear the DTC. Go to step 5.

Check the AUTO IDLE STOP DOES NOT OCCUR (A/C) in the PGM-FI SYSTEM with the HDS. Does the HDS indicate EXIST? Go to the climate control system's general troubleshooting information. When the climate control system troubleshooting is completed, clear the DTC. Go to step 6.

Check the IDLE STOP SW in the PGM-FI DATA LIST with the HDS. Does it indicate OPEN when the brake pedal is pressed and CLOSE when the brake pedal is released? The system is OK at this time. Go to step 20. - If CLOSE stays on, go to step 7. - If OPEN stays on, go to step 10.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Disconnect the idle stop switch 2P connector.

Check the IDLE STOP SW in the PGM-FI DATA LIST with the HDS. Does the HDS indicate OPEN? Replace the idle stop switch  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Braking Sensor/Switch/Service and Repair, then go to step 19. Repair a short to power in the wire between the idle stop switch 2P connector and the PCM (A13), then go to step 19.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Disconnect the idle stop switch 2P connector.
Connect idle stop switch 2P connector terminals No. 1 and No. 2 with a jumper wire.
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).

Check the IDLE STOP SW in the PGM-FI DATA LIST with the HDS. Does the HDS indicate CLOSE? Replace the idle stop switch  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Antilock Brakes / Traction Control Systems/Braking Sensor/Switch/Service and Repair, then go to step 19. Go to step 15.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Disconnect the jumper wire from the idle stop switch 2P connector.
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).

Measure the voltage between body ground and idle stop switch 2P connector terminal No. 1. Is there battery voltage? Repair an open in the wire between the idle stop switch 2P connector and the PCM (A13), then go to step 19. Repair an open in the wire between the No. B12 IG IMA (10 A) fuse and the idle stop switch 2P connector, then go to step 19.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Reconnect all connectors.
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).
Reset the PCM with the HDS.
Clear the DTC with the HDS.

Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P15C4 indicated? Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the PCM and the idle stop switch. Also check for brake system and climate control system DTCs, then go to step 1. is complete. If any other Pending or Confirmed DTCs were indicated, go to the indicated DTC's troubleshooting. With M/T P15C4 (118) DTC P15C4: Auto Idle Stop System Malfunction (M/T model)

Check the AUTO IDLE STOP DOES NOT OCCUR (BRAKE) in the PGM-FI SYSTEM DATA LIST with the HDS. Does the HDS indicate EXIST? Go to the brake booster pressure monitoring system's general troubleshooting information  See: Brakes and Traction Control/Power Brake Assist/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview. When the brake booster pressure monitoring system troubleshooting is completed, reset the ECM then clear the DTC in the IMA system. Go to step 2.

Check the AUTO IDLE STOP DOES NOT OCCUR (A/C) in the PGM-FI SYSTEM DATA LIST with the HDS. Does the HDS indicate EXIST? Go to the climate control system's general troubleshooting information  See: Heating and Air Conditioning/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview. When the climate control system troubleshooting is completed, reset the ECM then clear the DTC in the IMA system. Go to step 3.
Check the AUTO IDLE STOP DOES NOT OCCUR (NP SWITCH) in the PGM-FI SYSTEM DATA LIST with the HDS. Does the HDS indicate EXIST? Go to step 5. Go to step 4.

Check the AUTO IDLE STOP DOES NOT OCCUR (CLUTCH PEDAL POSITION SWITCH) in the PGM-FI SYSTEM DATA LIST with the HDS. Does the HDS indicate EXIST? Go to step 26. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Reset the ECM, then clear the DTC in the IMA system.

Check the NP SWITCH in the PGM-FI DATA LIST with the HDS. Does the HDS indicate ON when you move the shift lever to neutral, and does it indicate OFF when you move the shift lever to 1st or 2nd gear? Intermittent failure, the neutral position switch is OK at this time. Reset the ECM, and clear the DTC in the IMA system. - If the neutral position switch stays ON, go to step 6. - If the neutral position switch stays OFF, go to step 13. - If the indication is unstable, check the neutral position switch and the shift arm condition  See: Transmission and Drivetrain/Manual Transmission/Transaxle/Service and Repair/Overhaul/M/T Change Lever Assembly Disassembly/Reassembly, then go to step 67.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Disconnect the neutral position switch 2P connector.

Check the NP SWITCH in the PGM-FI DATA LIST with the HDS with the shift lever in neutral. Does the NP SWITCH indicate OFF? Replace the neutral position switch  See: Sensors and Switches/Sensors and Switches - Starting and Charging/Neutral Safety Switch/Service and Repair, then go to step 67. Go to step 9.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Jump the SCS line with the HDS.
Disconnect ECM connector B (44P).

Check for continuity between neutral position switch 2P connector terminal No. 2 and body ground. Is there continuity? Repair a short in the wire between the ECM (B13 and B21) and the neutral position switch, then go to step 67. Update the ECM if it does not have the latest software  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/ECM/PCM Update, then recheck. If the ECM was updated, replace the original ECM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair, then recheck.
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).
Disconnect the neutral position switch 2P connector.
Connect neutral position switch 2P connector terminal No. 2 to body ground with a jumper wire.
Check the NP SWITCH in the PGM-FI DATA LIST with the HDS. Does the HDS indicate ON? Go to step 17. Go to step 21.
Disconnect the jumper wire from the neutral position switch 2P connector.
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).
Connect neutral position switch 2P connector terminals No. 1 and No. 2 with a jumper wire.

Check the NP SWITCH in the PGM-FI DATA LIST with the HDS with the shift lever in neutral. Does the HDS indicate OFF? Repair an open in the wire between the neutral position switch and G101, then go to step 67. Replace the neutral position switch  See: Sensors and Switches/Sensors and Switches - Starting and Charging/Neutral Safety Switch/Service and Repair, then go to step 67.
Disconnect the jumper wire from the neutral position switch 2P connector.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Jump the SCS line with the HDS.
Disconnect ECM connector B (44P).

Check for continuity between these terminals: - ECM connector terminal B13 and neutral position switch 2P connector terminal No. 2. - ECM connector terminal B21 and neutral position switch 2P connector terminal No. 2. Is there continuity? Update the ECM if it does not have the latest software  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/ECM/PCM Update, then recheck. If the ECM was updated, replace the original ECM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair, then recheck. Repair an open in the wire between the neutral position switch and the ECM (B13 or B21), then go to step 67.

Check CLUTCH PEDAL POSITION SWITCH A and CLUTCH PEDAL POSITION SWITCH A (C) in the PGM-FI DATA LIST with the HDS. Do the HDS indicate CLOSE when the clutch pedal is pressed and OPEN when the clutch pedal is released? Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Reset the ECM, and clear the DTC in the IMA system. - If clutch pedal position switch A CLOSE stays on, go to step 27. - If clutch pedal position switch A OPEN stays on, go to step 34. - If clutch pedal position switch C CLOSE stays on, go to step 47. - If clutch pedal position switch C OPEN stays on, go to step 54. - If clutch pedal position switch A and clutch pedal position switch C OPEN stays on, repair an open in the wire between clutch pedal position switch A and C and G403, then go to step 67.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Disconnect the clutch pedal position switch A 2P connector.

Check CLUTCH PEDAL POSITION SWITCH A in the PGM-FI DATA LIST with the HDS. Does the HDS indicate OPEN? Replace clutch pedal position switch A  See: Sensors and Switches/Sensors and Switches - Starting and Charging/Clutch Switch/Service and Repair, then go to step 67. Go to step 30.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Jump the SCS line with the HDS.
Disconnect ECM connector B (44P).

Check for continuity between clutch pedal position switch A 2P connector terminal No. 1 and body ground. Is there continuity? Repair a short in the wire between the ECM (B12) and clutch pedal position switch A, then go to step 67. Update the ECM if it does not have the latest software  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/ECM/PCM Update, then recheck. If the ECM was updated, replace the original ECM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair, then recheck.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Disconnect clutch pedal position switch A 2P connector.
Connect clutch pedal position switch A 2P connector terminal No. 1 to body ground with jumper wire.
Check CLUTCH PEDAL POSITION SWITCH A in the PGM-FI DATA LIST with the HDS. Does the HDS indicate CLOSE? Go to step 38. Go to step 42.
Disconnect the jumper wire from the clutch pedal position switch A 2P connector.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Connect clutch pedal position switch A 2P connector terminals No. 1 and No. 2 with a jumper wire.

Check CLUTCH PEDAL POSITION SWITCH A in the PGM-FI DATA LIST with the HDS. Does the HDS indicate OPEN? Repair an open in the wire between clutch pedal position switch A and G403, then go to step 67. Replace clutch pedal position switch A  See: Sensors and Switches/Sensors and Switches - Starting and Charging/Clutch Switch/Service and Repair, then go to step 67.
Disconnect the jumper wire from the clutch pedal position switch A 2P connector.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Jump the SCS line with the HDS.
Disconnect ECM connector B (44P).

Check for continuity between clutch pedal position switch A 2P connector terminal No. 1 and ECM connector terminal B12. Is there continuity? Update the ECM if it does not have the latest software  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/ECM/PCM Update, then recheck. If the ECM was updated, replace the original ECM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair, then recheck. Repair an open in the wire between clutch pedal position switch A and the ECM (B12), then go to step 67.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Disconnect the clutch pedal position switch C 2P connector.

Check CLUTCH PEDAL POSITION SWITCH A (C) in the PGM-FI DATA LIST with the HDS. Does the HDS indicate OPEN? Replace clutch pedal position switch C  See: Sensors and Switches/Sensors and Switches - Starting and Charging/Clutch Switch/Service and Repair, then go to step 67. Go to step 50.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Jump the SCS line with the HDS.
Disconnect ECM connector B (44P).

Check for continuity between clutch pedal position switch C 2P connector terminal No. 1 and body ground. Is there continuity? Repair a short in the wire between the ECM (B39) and clutch pedal position switch C, then go to step 67. Update the ECM if it does not have the latest software  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/ECM/PCM Update, then recheck. If the ECM was updated, replace the original ECM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair, then recheck.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Disconnect the clutch pedal position switch C 2P connector.
Connect clutch pedal position switch C 2P connector terminal No. 1 to body ground with a jumper wire.
Check CLUTCH PEDAL POSITION SWITCH A (C) in the PGM-FI DATA LIST with the HDS. Does the HDS indicate CLOSE? Go to step 58. Go to step 62.
Disconnect the jumper wire from the clutch pedal position switch C 2P connector.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Connect clutch pedal position switch C 2P connector terminals No. 1 and No. 2 with a jumper wire.

Check CLUTCH PEDAL POSITION SWITCH A (C) in the PGM-FI DATA LIST with the HDS. Does the HDS indicate OPEN? Repair an open in the wire between clutch pedal position switch C and G403, then go to step 67. Replace clutch pedal position switch C  See: Sensors and Switches/Sensors and Switches - Starting and Charging/Clutch Switch/Service and Repair, then go to step 67.
Disconnect the jumper wire from the clutch pedal position switch C 2P connector.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Jump the SCS line with the HDS.
Disconnect ECM connector B (44P).

Check for continuity between clutch pedal position switch C 2P connector terminal No. 1 and ECM connector terminal B39. Is there continuity? Update the ECM if it does not have the latest software  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/ECM/PCM Update, then recheck. If the ECM was updated, replace the original ECM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair, then recheck. Repair an open in the wire between the clutch pedal position switch C and the ECM (B39), then go to step 67.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Reconnect all connectors.
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).
Reset the ECM with the HDS.
Clear the DTC with the HDS.

Do the auto idle stop system inspection  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Testing and Inspection/Component Tests and General Diagnostics/Auto Idle Stop System Inspection (CVT).

Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P15C4 indicated? Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the ECM and clutch pedal position switches A and C. Also check for brake system and climate control system DTCs, then go to step 1. is complete. If any other Pending or Confirmed DTCs are indicated, go to the indicated DTC's troubleshooting. Advanced Diagnostics Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P16C0: PCM CVT Control System Incomplete Update P16C0 DTC P16C0: PCM CVT Control System Incomplete Update
Update the PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/ECM/PCM Update.

Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P16C0 indicated? Replace the original PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair. is complete. Advanced Diagnostics Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P16C3 (31): DC-DC Converter Temperature Sensor Circuit Malfunction P16C3 (31) DTC P16C3: DC-DC Converter Temperature Sensor Circuit Malfunction
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).
Clear the DTC with the HDS.
Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC U1448, U1220, or U1221 indicated? Go to the indicated DTC's troubleshooting. Go to step 4.

Check the DC-DC CONVERTER TEMPERATURE in the DATA LIST with the HDS. Is more than 259 °F (126 °C), or less than -42 °F(-41 °C) indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check the IPU module air duct and the IPU module fan for disconnections, damage, or obstructions.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).

Check the IPU module air duct and the IPU module fan for disconnections, damage, or obstructions. Are the IPU module air duct and the IPU module fan OK? Replace the DC-DC converter  See: Hybrid Drive Systems/Power Control System/Voltage Converter/Service and Repair, then go to step 7. Repair or replace the IPU module air duct or the IPU module fan as needed, then go to step 7.
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).
Clear the DTC with the HDS.

Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P16C3 indicated? Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the DC-DC converter and the MCM, then go to step 1. is complete. If any other Pending or Confirmed DTCs are indicated, go to the indicated DTC's troubleshooting. Advanced Diagnostics Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P16D5: F-CAN Malfunction (Internal Malfunction) P16D5, P16D5 DTC P16D5: F-CAN Malfunction (Internal Malfunction)
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).
Clear the DTC with the HDS.

Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P16D5 indicated? Go to step 4. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. If any other Pending or Confirmed DTCs are indicated, go to the indicated DTC's troubleshooting.

Update the ECM/PCM if it does not have the latest software  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/ECM/PCM Update, or substitute a known-good ECM/PCM.

Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with HDS. Is DTC P16D5 indicated? If the ECM/PCM was updated, substitute a known-good ECM/PCM, then recheck. If the ECM/PCM was substituted, go to step 1. If the ECM/PCM was updated, troubleshooting is complete. If the ECM/PCM was substituted, replace the original ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair. If any other Pending or Confirmed DTCs are indicated, go to the indicated DTC's troubleshooting. Advanced Diagnostics Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P16D6: IMA-CAN Malfunction (Internal Malfunction) P16D6, P16D6 DTC P16D6: IMA-CAN Malfunction (Internal Malfunction)
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).
Clear the DTC with the HDS.

Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P16D6 indicated? Go to step 4. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. If any other Pending or Confirmed DTCs are indicated, go to the indicated DTC's troubleshooting.

Update the ECM/PCM if it does not have the latest software  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/ECM/PCM Update, or substitute a known-good ECM/PCM.

Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with HDS. Is DTC P16D6 indicated? If the ECM/PCM was updated, substitute a known-good ECM/PCM, then recheck. If the ECM/PCM was substituted, go to step 1. If the ECM/PCM was updated, troubleshooting is complete. If the ECM/PCM was substituted, replace the original ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair. If any other Pending or Confirmed DTCs are indicated, go to the indicated DTC's troubleshooting. Advanced Diagnostics Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P16D7: PCM Internal F-CAN Communication Circuit Malfunction P16D7 (107), P16D8 (120) DTC P16D7: PCM Internal F-CAN Communication Circuit Malfunction DTC P16D8: PCM Internal IMA-CAN Communication Circuit Malfunction
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).
Clear the DTC with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II), and wait for at least 5 seconds.

Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC U0029 or U0038 indicated? Go to the DTC U0029 troubleshooting  See: U Code Charts/U0029/DTC Troubleshooting/CVT System or the DTC U0038 troubleshooting  See: U Code Charts/U0038/CVT System. Go to step 6.
Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P16D7 or P16D8 indicated? Go to step 7. Intermittent failure, the system is OK this time.

Update the PCM if it does not have the latest software  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/ECM/PCM Update, or substitute a known-good PCM.
Start the engine, and let it idle for at least 2 minutes.

Monitor the OBD STATUS for P16D7 or P16D8 in the DTCs MENU with the HDS. Does the HDS indicate PASSED? If the PCM was updated, troubleshooting is complete.
