steps.instruction: Start the engine, and drive the vehicle at vehicle speed 25 mph (40 km/h) or more (CVT in D, M/T in 2nd or 3rd gear).

Keep the vehicle speed 25 mph (40 km/h) or more with the brake pedal pressed to switch ON the brake pedal position switch. - Drive the vehicle in this manner if the traffic regulations and ambient conditions allow. Diagnosis Details Conditions for setting the DTC When a malfunction is detected, the MIL comes on and a Pending DTC, a Confirmed DTC, and the freeze data are stored in the MCM memory. Conditions for clearing the DTC The MIL is cleared if the malfunction does not return in three consecutive trips in which the diagnostic runs. The MIL, the Pending DTC, the Confirmed DTC, and the freeze data can be cleared with the scan tool Clear command or by disconnecting the battery. P1634 (47) DTC P1634: Motor Power Inverter (MPI) Module Signal Circuit Malfunction
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).
Clear the DTC with the HDS.

Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P1634 indicated? Go to step 4. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the MCM and the MPI module.
Do the IPU MODULE FAN DRIVE in the INSPECTION MENU with the HDS. Does the HDS indicate OUT OF RANGE? Go to step 5. If the HDS indicates CONDITION IS NORMAL, go to step 15.

Check the No. B53 +B IMA 2 (15 A) fuse in the under-dash fuse/relay box. Is the fuse OK? Go to step 6. Repair a short to ground in the wire between the IPU module fan or the MPI module and the No. B53 IMA 2 (15 A) fuse. Also replace the No. B53 IMA 2 (15 A) fuse, then go to step 39.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Turn the battery module switch OFF.
Remove the IPU cover  See: Hybrid Drive Systems/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/IPU Cover Removal/Installation.
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II), and listen for a clicking sound from MCM relay 2 (A). Is there a clicking sound? Go to step 10. Go to step 27.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Remove MCM relay 2 (A).
Test MCM relay 2. Is the relay OK? Go to step 13. Replace MCM relay 2, then go to step 39.
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).

Measure the voltage between IPU relay holder (MCM relay 2) 8P connector terminal No. 2 and body ground. Is there battery voltage? Repair an open in the wire between MCM relay 2 and MPI module 12P connector terminal No. 7, then go to step 39. Repair an open in the wire between the No. B53 +B IMA 2 (15 A) fuse and MCM relay 2, then go to step 39.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Turn the battery module switch OFF.
Remove the IPU cover  See: Hybrid Drive Systems/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/IPU Cover Removal/Installation.
Remove the PCU lid, the PCU busplate, and the PCU cover  See: Hybrid Drive Systems/Power Control System/Drive Motor Control Module/Service and Repair.
Disconnect MCM connector C (22P).
Measure the resistance between MCM connector terminal C18 and body ground. Is there 1 MOhms or more? Go to step 21. Go to step 36.
Disconnect the MPI module 12P connector (A).
Reconnect MCM connector C (22P).
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).
Measure the voltage between MPI module 12P connector terminals No. 7 and No. 12. Is there about 12 V? Go to step 25. Go to step 38.

Measure the voltage between MPI module 12P connector terminal No. 2 and body ground. Is there about 5.0 V? Replace the MPI module  See: Hybrid Drive Systems/Power Control System/Voltage Inverter Module/Service and Repair, then go to step 39. Go to step 26.

Measure the voltage between MCM connector terminal C18 and body ground. Is there about 5.0 V? Repair an open in the wire between the MPI module 12P connector and the MCM (C18), then go to step 39. Go to step 45.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Remove MCM relay 2 (A).
Test MCM relay 2. Is the relay OK? Go to step 30. Replace MCM relay 2, then go to step 39.
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).

Measure the voltage between IPU relay holder (MCM relay 2) 8P connector terminal No. 3 and body ground. Is there about 12 V? Go to step 32. Repair an open in the wire between MCM relay 1 and MCM relay 2, then go to step 39.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Reinstall MCM relay 2 (A).
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).
Measure the voltage between MCM connector terminal A4 and body ground. Is there about 12 V? Go to step 45. Repair an open in the wire between MCM relay 2 and the MCM (A4), then go to step 39.
Disconnect the MPI module 12P connector (A).

Check for continuity between MCM connector terminal C18 and body ground. Is there continuity? Repair a short in the wire between the MPI module 12P connector and the MCM (C18), then go to step 39. Replace the MPI module  See: Hybrid Drive Systems/Power Control System/Voltage Inverter Module/Service and Repair, then go to step 39.

Measure the voltage between MPI module 12P connector terminal No. 7 and body ground. Is there battery voltage? Repair an open in the wire between MPI module 12P connector terminal No. 12 and G901, then go to step 39. Repair an open in the wire between MCM relay 2 and MPI module 12P connector terminal No. 7, then go to step 39.
Reconnect all connectors.
If removed, reinstall the PCU cover, the PCU busplate, and the PCU lid  See: Hybrid Drive Systems/Power Control System/Drive Motor Control Module/Service and Repair.
If removed, reinstall the IPU cover  See: Hybrid Drive Systems/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/IPU Cover Removal/Installation, then turn the battery module switch ON.
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).
Clear the DTC with the HDS.

Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P1634 indicated? Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the MCM and the MPI module, then go to step 1. is complete. If any other Pending or Confirmed DTCs are indicated, go to the indicated DTC's troubleshooting.
Reconnect all connectors.
Reinstall the PCU cover, the PCU busplate, and the PCU lid  See: Hybrid Drive Systems/Power Control System/Drive Motor Control Module/Service and Repair.
Reinstall the IPU cover  See: Hybrid Drive Systems/Service and Repair/Removal and Replacement/IPU Cover Removal/Installation, then turn the battery module switch ON.
Update the MCM if it does not have the latest software  See: Hybrid Drive Systems/Power Control System/Drive Motor Control Module/Testing and Inspection, or substitute a known-good MCM.
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).

Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P1634 indicated? Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the MCM and the MPI module. If the MCM was updated, substitute a known-good MCM, then recheck. If the MCM was substituted, go to step 1. If the MCM was updated, troubleshooting is complete. If the MCM was substituted, replace the original MCM  See: Hybrid Drive Systems/Power Control System/Drive Motor Control Module/Service and Repair. If any other Pending or Confirmed DTCs were indicated, go to the indicated DTC's . Advanced Diagnostics Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P1634 (47): Motor Power Inverter (MPI) Module Signal Circuit Malfunction P1658, P1658 DTC P1658: ETCS Control Relay ON Malfunction
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).

Do the ETCS TEST in the INSPECTION MENU with the HDS. Is the RELAY circuit OK? Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the ETCS control relay and the ECM/PCM. Go to step 3.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Open the fuse access panel (A), then remove the ETCS control relay (B) from the under-dash fuse/relay box.

Test the ETCS control relay  See: Power and Ground Distribution/Power Distribution Relay/Testing and Inspection. Is the ETCS control relay OK? Go to step 6. Replace the ETCS control relay, then go to step 13.
Jump the SCS line with the HDS.
Disconnect ECM/PCM connector A (44P).

Check for continuity between ECM/PCM connector terminal A20 and body ground. Is there continuity? Repair a short in the wire between the ECM/PCM(A20) and the ETCS control relay, then go to step 13. Go to step 9.
Disconnect ECM/PCM connector C (44P).
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).

Measure the voltage between ECM/PCM connector terminal C1 and body ground. Is there battery voltage? Repair a short to power in the wire between the ECM/PCM(C1) and the ETCS control relay, then go to step 12. Go to step 18.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Reconnect all connectors.
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).
Reset the ECM/PCM with the HDS.
Do the ECM/PCM idle learn procedure  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/ECM/PCM Idle Learn Procedure.

Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P1658 indicated? Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the ETCS control relay and the ECM/PCM, then go to step 1. is complete. If any other Pending or Confirmed DTCs are indicated, go to the indicated DTC's troubleshooting.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Reconnect all connectors.

Update the ECM/PCM if it does not have the latest software  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/ECM/PCM Update, or substitute a known-good ECM/PCM.

Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P1658 indicated? Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the ETCS control relay and the ECM/PCM. If the ECM/PCM was updated, substitute a known-good ECM/PCM, then recheck. If the ECM/PCM was substituted, go to step 1. If the ECM/PCM was updated, troubleshooting is complete. If the ECM/PCM was substituted, replace the original ECM/PCM  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Service and Repair. If any other Pending or Confirmed DTCs are indicated, go to the indicated DTC's troubleshooting. Advanced Diagnostics Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P1659: Electronic Throttle Control System (ETCS) Control Relay OFF Malfunction P1659, P1659 DTC P1659: ETCS Control Relay OFF Malfunction
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).
Clear the DTC with the HDS.

Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P1659 indicated? Go to step 4. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the ETCS control relay and the ECM/PCM.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Check the No. B52 DBW (ETCS) (15 A) fuse in the under-dash fuse/relay box. Is the fuse OK? Go to step 6. Go to step 19.

Test the ETCS control relay  See: Power and Ground Distribution/Power Distribution Relay/Testing and Inspection. Is the ETCS control relay OK? Go to step 8. Replace the ETCS control relay, then go to step 25.
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).
Measure the voltage between ETCS control relay 4P connector terminal No. 4 and body ground. Is there battery voltage? Go to step 10. Replace the under-dash fuse/relay box, then go to step 24.
Measure the voltage between ETCS control relay 4P connector terminal No. 2 and body ground. Is there battery voltage? Go to step 11. Replace the under-dash fuse/relay box, then go to step 24.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Jump the SCS line with the HDS.
Disconnect ECM/PCM connector C (44P).
Connect ETCS control relay 4P connector terminal No. 1 to body ground with a jumper wire.

Check for continuity between ECM/PCM connector terminal C1 and body ground. Is there continuity? Go to step 16. Repair an open in the wire between the ECM/PCM (C1) and the ETCS control relay, then go to step 25.
Disconnect ECM/PCM connector A (44P).
Connect ETCS control relay 4P connector terminal No. 3 to body ground with the jumper wire.

Check for continuity between ECM/PCM connector terminal A20 and body ground. Is there continuity? Go to step 30. Repair an open in the wire between the ECM/PCM (A20) and the ETCS control relay, then go to step 25.
Open the fuse access panel (A), then remove the ETCS control relay (B) from the under-dash fuse/relay box.
Jump the SCS line with the HDS.
Disconnect ECM/PCM connector C (44P).

Check for continuity between ECM/PCM connector terminal C1 and body ground. Is there continuity? Repair a short in the wire between the ECM/PCM (C1) and the ETCS control relay. Also replace the No. B52 DBW (ETCS) (15 A) fuse, then go to step 25. Go to step 23.
Check for continuity between ETCS control relay 4P connector terminal No. 2 and body ground. Is there continuity? Replace the under-dash fuse/relay box, then go to step 25. Go to step 30.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).
Reconnect all connectors.
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II).
Reset the ECM/PCM with the HDS.
Do the ECM/PCM idle learn procedure  See: Powertrain Management/Computers and Control Systems/Engine Control Module/Testing and Inspection/ECM/PCM Idle Learn Procedure.

Check for Pending or Confirmed DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC P1659 indicated? Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the ETCS control relay and the ECM/PCM, then go to step 1. is complete. If any other Pending or Confirmed DTCs are indicated, go to the indicated DTC's troubleshooting.

Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), and wait for at least 1 minute.

Test MCM relay 1. Is the relay OK? Repair a short to power in the wire between MCM relay 1 and the MCM (A9), then go to step 14. Replace MCM relay 1, then go to step 14.

Advanced Diagnostics Advanced Diagnostics - DTC P1898: CVT Drive Pulley Pressure Control Valve Stuck ON Or CVT Driven Pulley Pressure Control Valve Stuck OFF
Start the engine, and warm up the engine until the engine coolant temperature reaches 176 °F (80 °C).
Shift to D, and keep the brake pedal pressed for 5 seconds.

Drive the vehicle and accelerate to 41 - 49 mph (65 - 80 km/h) on a flat road and drive at least 15 seconds at 41 - 49 mph (65 - 80 km/h). (Do not release the accelerator pedal during the test-drive.)
Slow down and stop the vehicle, then keep the brake pedal pressed for 5 seconds.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0).

Repeat Driving Pattern steps 1 through 4. - Drive the vehicle in this manner only if the traffic regulations and ambient conditions allow. Diagnosis Details Conditions for setting the DTC When a malfunction is detected during the first drive cycle, a Pending DTC is stored in the PCM memory. If the malfunction returns in the next (second) drive cycle, the MIL comes on and a Confirmed DTC and the freeze data are stored. Conditions for clearing the DTC The MIL is cleared if the malfunction does not return in three consecutive trips in which the diagnostic runs. The MIL, the Pending DTC, the Confirmed DTC, and the freeze data can be cleared with the scan tool Clear command or by disconnecting the battery.
